mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the accommodation zone.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and forward economic momentum, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: expansion phase with labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, equity investors reprice growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month in same month
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or demand shock regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) does not explain incremental variance beyond single-series effects (R² increase < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors discount earnings under constrained production capacity.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the Fed faces a policy dilemma between inflation control and employment support. This regime-conditional interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate policy uncertainty and potential growth sacrifice.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint condition does not produce directionally consistent returns (sign accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,200 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand and forward economic activity. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as housing strength correlates with consumer confidence and construction employment.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher when HOUST > 1,200 (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of 0.3 percentage points or more signals deteriorating labor market conditions and forward recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings under weaker employment dynamics.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or early recession regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is in the lowest quartile of its historical distribution and retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month simultaneously, the economy faces demand-side weakness. This regime-conditional interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure from subdued consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 in lowest quartile AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect does not explain incremental variance beyond single-series effects (R² increase < 0.04).
